Before/After

Goal

Show change around a reference day.

Load Data

diary <- readr::read_csv("data/real/migraine_headache_diary.csv")
Rows: 4152 Columns: 5
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (2): medication, headache
dbl (2): participant_id, day
lgl (1): headache_reported

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Prepare Data

Day 0 is the reference day. We remove data after day 50 for illustration purposes only.

Data preparation step Functions in this chunk
Keep or remove rows filter()
Create or change columns mutate()
Count or summarize group_by(); summarize(); n() 
daily_summary <- diary |>
  filter(!is.na(day), day <= 50, !is.na(headache_reported)) |>
  mutate(period = if_else(day < 0, "Before day 0", "Day 0 and after")) |>
  group_by(day, period) |>
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    headache_percent = mean(headache_reported) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

period_means <- daily_summary |>
  group_by(period) |>
  summarize(
    mean_headache_percent = mean(headache_percent),
    x_start = min(day),
    x_end = max(day),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

daily_summary |>
  head(10)
# A tibble: 10 × 4
     day period           n headache_percent
   <dbl> <chr>        <int>            <dbl>
 1   -29 Before day 0    12             58.3
 2   -28 Before day 0    11             54.5
 3   -27 Before day 0    12             75  
 4   -26 Before day 0    12             75  
 5   -25 Before day 0    13             61.5
 6   -24 Before day 0    14             78.6
 7   -23 Before day 0    14             71.4
 8   -22 Before day 0    14             78.6
 9   -21 Before day 0    17             82.4
10   -20 Before day 0    19             52.6

Check n; small counts make percentages noisy.

Before And After Day 0

Plot component Code in this chunk
Aesthetic mappings x; y; color
Geoms / stats geom_line(); geom_point() 
p1 <- ggplot(daily_summary, aes(x = day, y = headache_percent, color = period)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 2)

p1
Plot component Code in this chunk
Geoms / stats geom_vline()
geom_vline() properties color; linetype
Scales / coordinates / facets / labels geom_segment(); annotate(); scale_color_manual(); labs(); theme_minimal(); theme() 
p2 <- p1 +
  geom_segment(
    data = period_means,
    aes(
      x = x_start,
      xend = x_end,
      y = mean_headache_percent,
      yend = mean_headache_percent,
      color = period
    ),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "gray50", linetype = "dashed") +
  annotate(
    geom = "label",
    x = 0,
    y = 88,
    label = "Day 0",
    fill = "white",
    color = "gray20",
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Before day 0" = "gray70", "Day 0 and after" = "#D55E00")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Headache Reporting Before And After Day 0",
    subtitle = "Horizontal lines are means before and after Day 0",
    x = "Diary day",
    y = "Headache reported (%)",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p2

This could represent a null finding: headache reporting looks similar before and after day 0.

Takeaways

  • Mark reference points directly on time plots.
  • A before/after plot can show little or no change.
